user_message: Just came out of escalation triage with Adatum Corporation. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 17:05 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 15:04 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 11:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-N trap (No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-N failure mode (No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-N -- No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
